This is an expensive place with no customer care. They have two persons to deal all the anger from the customers one is Mr.Danish slim guy AKA chota Danish and their CRO Wasif. Technicians and technical staff will ask you to pay them separately for creating mess with your vehicle. I have attached photos along with the review. I went there to replace the air conditioner due to a faulty compressor. It cost me 40k + 2k other part that they replaced they have team of 5 to 6 persons who deal with air conditioner they took whole day to install new unit and end of the day when they handed back my car its generator was not working it cost me another 4k to get a new battery becuse they forgot to adjust the generator. As new generator/Alternator was installed. This place is not recommended if you are not half mechanic. You will end up paying for their negligence. If you have a brand new car then it is fine to go their as the team is not expert and is very sharp at making excuses to get away with their own negligence. They use to have good people like Naveed, Zahid Khan but now they don't have expert professionals. Their two forman Asif and zeeshan are seniors but they'll be just sitting in the office all the time and if you ask to look at something they'll never move their butt. I am going their for last 5 years and two vehicles were maintained by them i have experienced several second visits, once they forgot to tight the tappet cover,  with my wagon r they were badly failed to diagnose the bearing issue. Like I said before if you have knowledge about your machine then it will be fine to go there. But avoid going there if you're not good enough to understand the mechanical of your car. Plus long wait is guaranteed.

All were fine except one guy who was rude with his own colleague.

Regards,
Daniyal Hussain

NOT Satisfied with the SERVICE DEPARTMENT; i had my vehicle diagnosed; the technician who started to diagnose the vehicle seemed like he was NOT happy to be part of the service department's team. He either was not experienced or did not think that satisfaction of the customer was important to him. Upon lifting the vehicle, first thing he told me to do is take the car back and have it washed underneath and then bring it back. His superior obviously disagreed with him and recommended to use a cleaner spray to clean the small area which was presenting the problem (a slow leaking oil pipe). From that point onwards till the end of the diagnosis, not only the technician DID NOT use the cleaner spray but also was not able to reach the loose clamp (the culprit of the problem). Throughout the process he did not remove the front wheel which was the right way to be able to reach the clamp easily but was not able to fix a simple clamp. The end of the story is that I had a simple mechanic at a mechanic '"workshop" who charged me a simple Rs. 500 (yes correct 5 hundred and even that I insisted because he was generous enough to resist charging me anything) to fix that clamp and was able to do this on a simple jack by removing the front wheel. At some parts of the world, the dealerships are known as "STEALERSHIPS" for this exact reason. Please step up ur game Honda Gateway and not become a stealership... since your inexperienced technician then proceeded to tell me to have the entire pump replaced until I asked him to get the head mechanic who obviously disagreed with the technician's diagnosis. Even though I was NOT charged by your service department, I still think I have to present an accurate picture on google reviews.
